catalyst: Activist 13D + insider cluster + 1-week volume anomaly (2.0x) at 43% discount to 52-wk high
catalyst_timing: Ongoing — activist pressure expected to build through Q3; volume accumulation just started
bull_case: EquipmentShare is a digitally-native equipment rental platform that insiders and an activist both see as undervalued at $20 vs $35.50 high. 3-signal convergence (highest in this scan). Activist 13D compels public disclosure of intent to create shareholder value — M&A or operational improvements could re-rate the stock. Volume anomaly (Jun 26: 5.4M vs 2M baseline) confirms accumulation is active right now.
bear_case: PE of 5,323 means essentially no near-term earnings support. Equipment rental is cyclical and sensitive to construction slowdowns. Activist may face management resistance. Price already bounced from May 5 low ($17.95) to $20.23.
priced_in_judgment: Partially: the volume spike and small price bounce suggest some awareness. But activist narrative typically takes weeks to fully price in — most of the upside should still be ahead.

catalyst: FRESH activist 13D (last 14 days) + insider buying exactly at 52-wk low ($13.98 June 22) + volume accumulation (1.67x)
catalyst_timing: Catalyst building now — activist disclosed 5%+ stake within last 14 days; thesis resolution over next 4-8 weeks as activist publishes demands
bull_case: Alkami Technology is a digital banking SaaS platform at $1.8B market cap. The FRESH activist 13D is the most timely signal in this scan — someone just disclosed a 5%+ stake and intent to change the company, right when insiders were also buying at the June 22 52-week low ($13.98). That double-commitment at maximum pessimism is the classic setup. Price has recovered 21.6% already but is still 45% below the $30.88 high. Volume (1.67x) confirms ongoing accumulation. Activist could push for a strategic sale or operational overhaul of an undervalued SaaS franchise.
bear_case: Stock already bounced 21.6% from the $13.98 low — some of the easy money is made. Unprofitable (PE -35.7). Activist could be a short-term speculator, not a committed change agent. Digital banking is crowded. If the activist 13D was already known to the market, the bounce is the signal.
priced_in_judgment: Only partially: $13.98 → $17.00 (+21.6%) is the market just waking up; the activist angle is still fresh and the full re-rating (back toward the $22-25 range) hasn't happened yet.

catalyst: Smart money holders + volume anomaly (1.84x) + short squeeze — BUT pre-market showing -24% gap down (bid $18.70) suggesting another negative catalyst today (Jun 30)
catalyst_timing: Broken thesis — additional negative news appears to be hitting today (Jun 30 pre-market)
bull_case: If the pre-market gap down is overdone and CNXC stabilizes, a technical bounce from oversold could produce 8-12% intraday. Dividend yield of 5.6% provides some support.
bear_case: CRITICAL BEAR: Pre-market trading at ~$19.19 vs $25.23 close = -24% gap down. This follows the June 18 'guidance bomb' (9.5M volume spike, -14% day). Another major negative catalyst appearing today means the fundamental thesis is deteriorating rapidly. Smart money may be wrong or already exiting. Do NOT enter this name.
priced_in_judgment: Fully: the signals are stale relative to today's apparent bad news. The market is pricing in ongoing fundamental deterioration at Concentrix.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + short squeeze setup (>20% short float) on profitable consumer staples stock at 10.8x PE
catalyst_timing: No specific date catalyst — thesis builds over the week as short covering accelerates
bull_case: Vital Farms (pasture-raised eggs/dairy) is one of the few names in this scan that is actually profitable (PE 10.8x), trading at 79% below its 52-week high ($53.13) — an extreme dislocation. Insiders are buying, suggesting management sees near-term recovery. Short float >20% provides squeeze fuel. Consumer staples demand is durable regardless of macro. The June 18 volume spike (8.8M = 4.4x normal) shows a big move was already triggered — another catalyst could ignite again.
bear_case: Down 79% from high suggests a fundamental problem (likely bird flu supply chain disruption). If the underlying issue (egg supply normalization) is still ongoing, insiders could be wrong about timing. Market cap $485M limits institutional FOMO.
priced_in_judgment: Only partially priced in — insiders buying at current levels suggests they see the current price as materially below fair value. Short squeeze hasn't fired yet.

catalyst: Earnings beat (Q1 FY2027) + insider buying cluster — CarMax beat analyst estimates, stock ran +13% on Jun 18 but insiders continue to accumulate
catalyst_timing: Earnings catalyst already fired (Jun 18). Now riding post-earnings momentum window (typically 2-3 weeks of sustained buying after a strong beat)
bull_case: CarMax beat earnings and the stock validated the insider signal. Post-earnings drift is a well-documented phenomenon — stocks that beat significantly continue to outperform for 3-4 weeks. Insiders were positioned before the beat, suggesting they see more than one quarter of upside. PE 34.7x is reasonable for a market leader recovering from $30.26 lows.
bear_case: Earnings catalyst already reflected in price — stock ran from $47.43 to $53.66 on earnings day. Post-earnings drift could be exhausted. $7.5B market cap means it needs large capital flows to move. No specific catalyst for next week.
priced_in_judgment: Mostly priced in — earnings beat is now public knowledge. Insider signal adds credibility but the initial move has been made.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + short squeeze (>20% short float) with June 26 volume spike (3.4x) — Better Home & Finance online mortgage platform
catalyst_timing: No specific date — squeeze dynamic builds over the week if momentum continues
bull_case: Better Home & Finance (online mortgage platform) has a >20% short float and insiders buying at lows. The June 26 volume spike (1.0M vs 290K baseline = 3.4x) is notable accumulation. Short squeeze potential is high with such a thin float (7.5M shares). If mortgage rates ease, the sector could get a quick re-rating.
bear_case: Extremely wide pre-market spread (bid $24, ask $33.65) = illiquid and dangerous for execution. PE -2.26 = money-losing mortgage lender in a high-rate environment. 52-week high $94.06 = stock has already been crushed. $15M float makes it a meme stock risk — can reverse violently.
priced_in_judgment: Not fully priced in — June 26 spike just started accumulation. But illiquidity makes entry and exit very risky with real capital.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + short squeeze on pharma compounding company with near-term ophthalmic drug launch catalysts
catalyst_timing: Catalysts expected Q3 2026 — FDA actions and new product launches in ophthalmic formulations
bull_case: Harrow (pharma compounding, ophthalmic focus) has been running since June 10 ($33.46 → $41.15 = +23%) driven by insider buying + short squeeze dynamics. A prior scan identified this as a winner for July 1 catalysts. The continued insider accumulation suggests management sees near-term catalysts ahead. Short squeeze fuel remains with >20% short float. Ophthalmic compounding is a defensible niche market.
bear_case: Already ran +23% from lows — the easy money may be made. PE -100.7 = heavily unprofitable. If the July 1 catalyst from the prior scan doesn't materialize, the squeeze could unwind.
priced_in_judgment: Partially — the run from $33.46 to $41.15 is the market recognizing something. But if there's a true pipeline catalyst, significant further upside remains.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + smart money holding on below-book-value packaging company with July 7 dividend payment
catalyst_timing: Dividend payment on July 7 (exactly next Monday entry date) provides income buyer support
bull_case: Graphic Packaging trades below book value (PB 0.97) with an 11.6x PE and 4.1% dividend yield. Insider buying + smart money agreement that this is undervalued. Dividend payable July 7 means income buyers will be accumulating this week. $3.2B stable packaging business trading at 55% below its $23.76 52-week high.
bear_case: Down 55% from high suggests structural headwinds in paper packaging (plastic alternatives, volume declines). $3.2B market cap requires large capital flows to move meaningfully in one week. Low beta = boring trade.
priced_in_judgment: Insiders and smart money are accumulating but there's no near-term catalyst beyond the dividend and valuation support. Most of the thesis is already in the price.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + smart money interest in insurance disruptor with strong momentum run (+42% from May 28 lows)
catalyst_timing: No specific near-term catalyst — riding momentum from May 28 lows
bull_case: Goosehead Insurance has broken out +42% from its $33.68 low (May 28) to $47.98 in ~30 days. Insiders and smart money both present. Independent insurance brokers benefit from market disruption in personal lines. Volume trending up: Jun 18 (1.3M), Jun 22 (801K), Jun 26 (1.0M). PE 41.8x reflects growth expectations.
bear_case: Already ran +42% in 30 days — momentum could be exhausted. PE 41.8x is expensive for an insurance broker. 52-week high is $106.84 so there's theoretical upside but near-term momentum may be crowded.
priced_in_judgment: Partially: the momentum run suggests the market is waking up, but the $47.98 price is still 55% below the 52-week high.

catalyst: Insider buying cluster + short squeeze (>20% short float) on Weight Watchers turnaround candidate
catalyst_timing: No specific date catalyst — squeeze builds if momentum continues
bull_case: WW International has a >20% short float and insiders buying at lows. The GLP-1 drug narrative could be fading, allowing WW's behavioral program to reclaim market share. PE essentially 0 suggests potential profitability recovery. Short float makes it a squeeze target.
bear_case: Extremely illiquid: ~200K daily volume and $163M market cap. Very wide spreads. The GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) narrative has crushed WW — insiders may be too early. Very difficult to exit quickly with real capital.
priced_in_judgment: Not fully priced in from a short squeeze perspective, but fundamental thesis is very weak.
